evals.name: init-scaffold
evals.expected_output: Lead detects the init route from the first word of arguments. Loads references/init.md. Invokes forge-init.sh against the working directory. The script either creates the 5 scaffolded files (fresh dir) or reports all 5 as preserved (idempotent rerun). Lead relays the script's output to the user.
expectations: References/init.md is loaded by the lead (visible as a Read tool call)
forge-init.sh is invoked via Bash
The script output mentions all five expected scaffold targets: .gitignore, README.md, playwright.config.ts, .env, hints/README.md
If the target directory already had forge/ scaffolded, all five files are reported as 'Preserved (already present)' — idempotency holds
Lead's final user-facing report includes the script's output verbatim or near-verbatim
No team-task content is loaded (init route does not pull in driver/snippet-author orchestration)

evals.name: first-drive-authors-novel
evals.prompt: /forge log in then sort the inventory page by price low-to-high
evals.expected_output: Lead routes to drive mode (task fallthrough; no spec-authoring intent in the task). Loads references/team-task.md. Spawns 2 teammates (driver + snippet-author). Driver invokes the existing login-as-persona snippet, then drives the sort step fresh. Snippet-author writes ≥1 new snippet file capturing the sort step. Final report acknowledges the new snippet(s).
expectations: Lead declares MODE=drive in its planning output
References/team-task.md is loaded; references/init.md and references/export.md are NOT loaded
Exactly 2 Agent (subagent) calls are made: one for forge:driver and one for forge:snippet-author
No Agent call is made for forge:spec-writer or forge:spec-verifier
At least one new .ts file appears under forge/snippets/ that did not exist before the run
Each newly-written snippet has a meta block with description, args, and (where relevant) envKeys fields
Lead's final report says 'Snippet-author wrote N snippet(s)' with N ≥ 1, naming the new snippets

evals.name: second-drive-reuses-library
evals.prompt: /forge add the backpack to cart and report the badge count
evals.expected_output: Lead routes to drive mode. Spawns driver + snippet-author. Driver scans forge/snippets/, finds login-as-persona, add-item-to-cart, and cart-get-badge-count all match by intent, and invokes them rather than driving fresh. Snippet-author skips all chunks (all 'invoked', none 'drove fresh') and writes zero new files. Final report says 'Snippet-author wrote 0 snippets — drive's work was covered by existing library.'
expectations: Lead declares MODE=drive in its planning output
Exactly 2 Agent calls: forge:driver and forge:snippet-author
No Agent call for forge:spec-writer or forge:spec-verifier
Driver's SendMessages to snippet-author include at least one 'invoked' marker (visible in the summary field)
Zero new files are written to forge/snippets/ during the run (file count before == file count after)
Lead's final report says 'Snippet-author wrote 0 snippets' or semantically equivalent (e.g. 'no new snippets — drive's work was covered by existing library')

evals.name: spec-end-to-end-recorded
evals.prompt: /forge spec add the backpack to cart and verify badge count is 1, record as before
evals.expected_output: Lead routes to spec mode (first word is 'spec'). Captures RECORD_AS=before. Loads references/team-task.md. Spawns all 4 teammates. Driver runs the task, snippet-author either invokes or authors as appropriate, spec-writer composes a .spec.ts in forge/specs/, spec-verifier runs forge-pool-run-spec.mjs with --record-as before. Spec passes from cold start. Video persists at forge/videos/<spec-basename>-before.webm. Final report includes spec path + video path.
expectations: Lead declares MODE=spec and RECORD_AS=before in its planning
Exactly 4 Agent calls: forge:driver, forge:snippet-author, forge:spec-writer, forge:spec-verifier
A new .spec.ts file is created in forge/specs/ during the run
The new spec file contains zero occurrences of the literal strings 'secret_sauce', 'standard_user', or 'problem_user' (credentials must come from process.env)
The new spec file contains at least one import statement of the form `from '../snippets/...'` (snippets are imported, not inlined)
Spec-verifier invokes forge-pool-run-spec.mjs with both --record and --record-as before
Spec-verifier's run exits 0 (spec passes from cold start)
A video file exists at forge/videos/<spec-basename>-before.webm after the run
The video file is larger than 10000 bytes (not an empty recording)
Lead's final report includes both the spec filename and the video path

evals.name: export-inlined
evals.expected_output: Lead routes to export. Loads references/export.md. Invokes forge-export-spec.mjs --spec <forge>/specs/add-backpack-to-cart-standard.spec.ts --output <project>/forge-exports/add-backpack-to-cart-standard.spec.ts --force. Result is a self-contained spec that no longer imports from forge/snippets/. Final report includes the export path.
expectations: References/export.md is loaded; references/team-task.md and references/init.md are NOT loaded
forge-export-spec.mjs is invoked via Bash with --spec, --output, and --force flags
A file exists at <project-root>/forge-exports/add-backpack-to-cart-standard.spec.ts after the run
The exported file does NOT contain any `from '../snippets/'` import statements (snippet bodies are inlined)
The exported file's only external import is from '@playwright/test' (self-contained for shipping)
Lead's final report includes the absolute path of the exported file

evals.name: mode-detect-spec-natural
evals.prompt: /forge create a spec for adding the backpack to the cart and verifying the badge count
evals.expected_output: Lead routes to task (first word is not 'spec', 'init', or 'export'). Phase 0a's natural-language detection picks up 'create a spec' and sets MODE=spec. Downstream behavior matches spec mode: 4 teammates spawned, spec produced and verified.
expectations: Lead declares MODE=spec in its planning output despite the first word not being 'spec'
References/team-task.md is loaded
Exactly 4 Agent calls: forge:driver, forge:snippet-author, forge:spec-writer, forge:spec-verifier
A new .spec.ts file is created in forge/specs/
Spec-verifier runs the spec; it passes (exit 0)

evals.name: mode-detect-init-natural-PENDING-PHASE0
evals.expected_output: PENDING — currently fails. The desired behavior is that natural-language phrasings like 'install forge here', 'scaffold forge', 'set up forge in this project' route to the init scaffolder rather than falling into drive mode. Will pass after Phase 0 is expanded to natural-language-detect the init route.
expectations: Lead detects the init route via natural language (not drive mode)
References/init.md is loaded
forge-init.sh is invoked via Bash
No team-task content (driver, snippet-author orchestration) is loaded

evals.name: mode-detect-export-natural-PENDING-PHASE0
evals.prompt: /forge export the spec from the latest recording
evals.expected_output: PENDING — currently fails. The desired behavior is that natural-language phrasings like 'export the spec from the latest recording' route to the spec exporter rather than falling into drive mode. Will pass after Phase 0 is expanded to natural-language-detect the export route. The lead may use AskUserQuestion to disambiguate which spec the user means.
expectations: Lead detects the export route via natural language (not drive mode)
References/export.md is loaded
Either forge-export-spec.mjs is invoked, OR the lead uses AskUserQuestion to confirm which spec to export before invoking
No team-task content is loaded

evals.name: mode-detect-bare-task
evals.prompt: /forge log in as standard_user and tell me what's on the inventory page
evals.expected_output: Bare drive task with no spec-authoring intent. Lead routes to task fallthrough and selects MODE=drive (default). 2 teammates spawned; no spec written.
expectations: Lead declares MODE=drive in its planning
Exactly 2 Agent calls: forge:driver and forge:snippet-author
No Agent call for forge:spec-writer or forge:spec-verifier
No new .spec.ts file appears in forge/specs/ during the run

evals.name: mode-detect-incidental-spec
evals.prompt: /forge add the backpack to cart. We just need this done — no spec writing or test creation needed.
evals.expected_output: Task contains the word 'spec' but in an incidental/negating context (the user explicitly says not to write a spec). Lead correctly selects MODE=drive despite the word appearing. Tests that Phase 0a's natural-language detection doesn't naively keyword-match.
expectations: Lead declares MODE=drive — the 'spec' mention is incidental and explicitly negated
Exactly 2 Agent calls (driver + snippet-author only)
No Agent call for forge:spec-writer or forge:spec-verifier
No new .spec.ts file appears in forge/specs/ during the run

evals.name: record-keyword-in-drive-stays-drive
evals.prompt: /forge add the backpack to cart and record the badge count for me
evals.expected_output: Task uses the word 'record' but as colloquial 'report' / 'note down', not as a recording-flag request. Lead correctly selects MODE=drive — the recording-label detection in Phase 0a only runs in spec mode, and 'record the badge count' doesn't match the spec-intent patterns. No video should be produced.
expectations: Lead declares MODE=drive (the word 'record' does not elevate to spec)
Exactly 2 Agent calls: forge:driver and forge:snippet-author
No video file is created in forge/videos/ during the run
No .spec.ts file is created in forge/specs/ during the run

evals.name: spec-mode-default-recording-name
evals.prompt: /forge spec verify the cart badge increments after adding the backpack
evals.expected_output: Spec mode (first-word 'spec') with NO 'record as' phrase. RECORD_AS=none. Spec-verifier records with the default timestamp filename. The resulting video preserves the spec-basename prefix (regression check for the recent naming fix).
expectations: Lead declares MODE=spec and RECORD_AS=none
Exactly 4 Agent calls: forge:driver, forge:snippet-author, forge:spec-writer, forge:spec-verifier
Spec-verifier invokes forge-pool-run-spec.mjs with --record but WITHOUT --record-as
A video file exists in forge/videos/ after the run
The video filename matches the pattern <spec-basename>-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.webm (timestamp suffix, spec-basename prefix preserved)
The video filename does NOT start with just the timestamp — the spec-basename prefix must be present
